current.title: LinkMap Agent — Codex-Powered Browser Agent (Plan v1)
current.description: BLOCKER to probe: codex issue #24135: — headless `codex exec` auto-cancels MCP tool calls (closed stdin ≈ approval rejection); the documented workaround is `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, unacceptable per `codex-worker-safety.md`. Must live-probe on our 0.144.1 before lock-in. Fallback: long-lived interactive codex behind a pty (node-pty), also unproven on Windows.; Bridge transport: side-panel-owned WebSocket: to `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` — zero manifest changes, sidesteps MV3's 30s SW kill (panel is a long-lived DOM page). Native messaging rejected (per-connection host process fights a daemon; registry install; 1MB cap; stdio framing bugs) but kept as future out-of-band token channel. **Non-negotiable auth:* bind loopback only, verify `Origin === chrome-extension://<id>`, mutual challenge-response pairing token (any web page can probe loopback WS; port-squatting is real).; Started: 2026-07-18
